data.description:

data.title: Stablecoins Gain Traction as Financial Giants Explore Digital Tokens
data.description:
According to PANews, stablecoins are increasingly becoming mainstream, with banks and credit card companies exploring the issuance of their own digital tokens. This trend could potentially reshape the flow of funds in the United States and globally.

Jose Fernandez da Ponte, PayPal's Senior Vice President of Blockchain, Cryptocurrency, and Digital Currency, emphasized that stablecoins are not merely a means of transferring value but have evolved into a foundational infrastructure layer in many instances.

Mastercard announced this week that its Multi-Token Network will support four types of stablecoins, marking a significant step in integrating digital currencies into traditional financial systems.

Visa's CEO revealed efforts to modernize its infrastructure using stablecoins, indicating a strategic move towards embracing digital currency technology.

Additionally, JPMorgan has introduced a token backed by commercial bank deposits rather than the U.S. dollar, showcasing a novel approach to digital token issuance.

data.title: Stratis Launches StraxTegic to Help Enterprises Customize Blockchain Solutions
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Stratis' official X platform, Stratis has launched a business service called StraxTegic, which is designed to help companies navigate the Web3 ecosystem. The service provides customized blockchain strategies, professional development based on .NET Core, and comprehensive compliance support. StraxTegic is committed to turning innovative ideas into practical solutions, helping companies from conception to implementation. In addition, Stratis also emphasized that its blockchain platform is not only a blockchain, but also a launch pad for innovation. Its StratisEVM is secured by STRAX tokens, enabling companies to expand without compromise.

data.title: Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller Piles $146,000,000 Into Two Assets, Betting on Digital Transformation and Global Gaming
data.description:

data.title: Ethiopia sees surge in power concerns despite crypto mining boom
data.description:
Ethiopia has seen a rise in power concerns attributed to crypto mining activities amid the boom in the country’s cryptocurrency industry. According to reports, the crypto mining and data center industry in the country is expected to consume a third of the country’s electricity supply in 2025. This forecast has raised issues about the allocation […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ethiopia-power-concerns-crypto-mining-boom/

data.title: Binance Alpha has launched Rekt (REKT), and users who hold at least 170 Binance Alpha points can participate in the airdrop
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Binance Alpha has now launched Rekt (REKT). Eligible Binance users can use Binance Alpha points to claim 140,230,000 REKT tokens airdrop on the Alpha event page within 24 hours after the start of trading. Binance Alpha airdrops will adopt a first-come, first-served model: users with at least 170 Binance Alpha points can participate in the airdrop, first come, first served, until the airdrop pool is full or the event ends.
Please note that claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Binance Alpha points. Users must confirm the claim within 24 hours on the Alpha event page, otherwise it will be deemed to have given up the airdrop.
